_comment: Step 1: Initial state — empty blue gauge. Gauge shows empty fill bar with blue (#4A9EFF) severity indicator and System label.
_verify: Fill bar height is 0px (no visible fill)
Severity indicator color is #4A9EFF (info blue, from token color.severity.info)
Label text reads System
Background frame uses token-driven backdrop color (dark, not white)
No animation — immediate render from idle state

_comment: Step 2: Animate to 75% — smooth fill-up animation over 300ms.
_verify: Fill bar smoothly rises from 0% to 75% of track height over ~300ms
No jump or stutter — linear interpolation visible
Severity indicator stays blue (info unchanged)
Label still reads System
Animation completes and rests at 75%

_comment: Step 3: Severity snap info to warning — indicator snaps to #FFB800 amber immediately. Fill stays at 75%.
_verify: Severity indicator color snaps immediately to #FFB800 (warning amber)
NO color fade or transition — snap is instantaneous
Fill bar remains at 75% (level unchanged)
Label still reads System
Change is immediate — no delay after publish

_comment: Step 4: Animate to 95% — near-full, tension builds. Smooth rise from 75% to 95% over 300ms with amber indicator.
_verify: Fill bar smoothly rises from 75% to 95% of track height over ~300ms
Severity indicator stays amber (warning unchanged)
Label still reads System
Animation is smooth — no stutter
At rest: fill bar is clearly near-full (very small gap at top)

_comment: Step 5: Critical alarm — both severity=error and label=CRITICAL snap simultaneously. Fill stays at 95%.
_verify: Severity indicator snaps immediately to #FF4444 (error red)
Label text snaps immediately to CRITICAL
NO transitions on either change — both snap
Fill bar remains at 95% (level unchanged from step 4)
Final state: 95% fill, red indicator, CRITICAL label — maximum alarm visual
